heading: Benefits in certain cases of in-service or service-connected deaths
label: 1312 Benefits in certain cases of in-service or service-connected deaths
notes: References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (a), is , . Title II of the Social Security Act is classified generally to subchapter II (§ 401 et seq.) of chapter 7 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. Sections 214 and 202 of the Social Security Act are classified to sections 414 and 402 of Title 42, respectively. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act Aug. 14, 1935, ch. 53149 Stat. 620section 1305 of Title 42

L. 87–268, § 375 Stat. 566Sept. 21, 1961“The amendments made by this Act [amending this section and sections 107, 411 [now 1311], 415 [now 1315], 422 [now 1322], and 503 [now 1503] of this title and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section] shall take effect as of the first day of the first calendar month which begins after the date of its enactment [].”Sept. 21, 1961

Filing of Application for Benefits, , , provided that the increased pension benefits authorized by were to be payable from the effective date of to anyone receiving dependency and indemnity compensation on such date only if the application for such increased benefits were filed with the Veterans’ Administration within one year from such date and evidence of entitlement were of record or received within one year from the date of request therefor.Pub. children.text: who dies after , and is not a fully and currently insured individual (as defined in section 214 of the Social Security Act ()) at the time of such veteran’s death; andDecember 31, 195642 U.S.C. 414

children.label: A
children.text: while on active duty, active duty for training, or inactive duty training; or

children.label: B
children.text: as the result of a service-connected disability incurred after ; andSeptember 15, 1940

who leaves one or more survivors who are not entitled for any month to monthly benefits under section 202 of the Social Security Act () on the basis of such veteran’s wages and self-employment income but who would, upon application therefor, be entitled to such benefits if such veteran had been fully and currently insured at the time of such veteran’s death;42 U.S.C. 402

children.label: b
children.text: In any case where the amount of dependency and indemnity compensation payable under this chapter to a surviving spouse who has children is less than the amount of pension which would be payable to (1) such surviving spouse, or (2) such children if the surviving spouse were not entitled, under chapter 15 of this title had the death occurred under circumstances authorizing payment of death pension, the Secretary shall pay dependency and indemnity compensation to such surviving spouse in an amount equal to such amount of pension.
